CheckTitle: Ensure FIDO2 or passwordless authentication is enabled
Description: Checks whether FIDO2 security keys or Microsoft Authenticator passwordless sign-in are enabled in the tenant authentication methods policy. Passwordless methods are the strongest form of authentication and resist phishing attacks.
Risk: Without passwordless methods enabled, users must rely on passwords plus traditional MFA which remain vulnerable to adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) phishing attacks. FIDO2 and passwordless are phishing-resistant by design.

Remediation.Recommendation: Enable FIDO2 security keys and/or Microsoft Authenticator passwordless sign-in in the authentication methods policy. Start with a pilot group of admins and expand to all users.

Frameworks.source: AI-generated mapping — not yet verified against published benchmark

SourceNotes: Phishing-resistant MFA is a CISA priority. CIS control ID is AI-generated.
